questions.text: Which HTML element is used to define the main heading of a page?

feedback.cita_literal: The <h1> element represents a top-level heading. There should be only one <h1> per page, representing the primary topic.
feedback.explicacion_fallo: h1 is the correct top-level heading element. <header> is a sectioning element, <title> sets the browser tab text (not visible on the page), and <head> contains metadata.

questions.text: Which CSS property is used to change the text color of an element?

feedback.cita_literal: The color CSS property sets the foreground color value of an element's text and text decorations.
feedback.explicacion_fallo: The 'color' property sets the text color. 'background-color' changes the background, 'font-color' does not exist in CSS, and 'text-style' is not a valid CSS property.

questions.text: What does the JavaScript method document.getElementById() return when no matching element is found?

feedback.cita_literal: Returns a reference to the Element object, or null if an element with the specified ID is not found in the document.
feedback.explicacion_fallo: getElementById() returns null when no element matches, not undefined, an empty string, or false. This is important for null-checking before using the returned value.

questions.text: Which HTTP status code indicates that a resource was not found on the server?
questions.articulo_referencia: RFC 7231 — HTTP/1.1 Semantics and Content, Section 6.5.4

feedback.cita_literal: The 404 (Not Found) status code indicates that the origin server did not find a current representation for the target resource.
feedback.explicacion_fallo: 404 means 'Not Found'. 200 means the request succeeded, 301 indicates a permanent redirect, and 500 signals an internal server error.
